value.text: The Kingdom of Bohemia (Czech: České království),[a] sometimes referenced in English literature as the Czech Kingdom,[a][8][9] was a medieval and early modern monarchy in Central Europe. It was the predecessor state of the modern Czech Republic. [...] [T]he kingdom was formally established (by elevating Duchy of Bohemia) in 1198 by Přemysl Ottokar I [...].
